test_suite.details: node test.js: 3359 passed, 0 failed. Covers wins.json schema, HTML output consistency, internal links, build pipeline, data cross-references, sections.json schema, prose integrity, and 77 prediction panels (12 tombstone, 65 mined, 10 operational).

internal_anchors.details: Test suite covers internal href='#id' anchors and showTab targets. Spot-verified tab IDs: 12 tabs with unique IDs; all showTab() targets resolve to real tab-content divs. All base WINs have win<NNN> anchors.

tab_structure.details: 12 tab-content divs (overview, evaluate, model, selftest, wins, pages, killshots, timestamp, predictions, falsify, ai, refs). Each has exactly one section-nav block; chain is continuous prev/next; first prev is null, last next is null. Active on load = overview. Heading hierarchy valid (every tab has h1; h2s nest under it).

external_links.details: 118 external URLs extracted; 60 are non-citation (data source / tool / institutional). Spot-checked 10 priority data-source URLs (NOAA NCEI, INTERMAGNET, ESA Gaia, GRACE-FO, PODAAC, BGS INTERMAGNET node, ESA Swarm, HeartMath, NGDC, NOAA main). All resolve (some via 301 redirect to final 200; noaa.gov main returns 403 to curl UA but serves humans normally — not a real failure). Citation/DOI links skipped per agent scope.

data_prose_consistency.details: No unrendered placeholders. Scorecard: base WINs=69 (matches 3-digit ID filter in build); verdicts Refuted=9, Self-Contradicted=14, Misleading=22, StdModel=18, NotDemo=4, Unfalsifiable=4 (tallied across all 71 entries including sub-IDs 057b,058b — intentional, matches computeVerdictTallies over full wins.json). Honest-accuracy prose: 69/(69+4+4) = 89.6% matches HTML. 8 failures counted in prose matches entries.length in uncounted-failures.json. code_analysis: 69 base reviewed / 0 pending; hardcoded=26, live_fetch=3, none=40; relabels_standard=49, post_hoc=67, derives_from_dome=2 — all match HTML.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 69 base WINs (and sub-IDs 057b, 058b) have win<NNN>/win<NNN>b anchor IDs. 8 detail_groups defined; all members cross-reference each other. One minor: detail_group label 'WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063' does not mention sub-ID 057b which is also in that group — label is descriptive shorthand, 057b is a tracking entry for a dome numbering collision, cosmetic only. All WINs have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields.

win_number_alignment.details: Dome site wins.html still shows 69 confirmed + PROS-001 through PROS-008 prospective. Our wins.json base count = 69 (matches). Spot-checked 7 WIN titles (001 Tesla 11.78Hz, 004 SAA Exponential, 007 NP post-1990, 022 1990 Phase Transition, 024 Roaring 40s=SAA boundary, 025 2024 Eclipse 9-station, 029 Schumann ceiling) — all match. Dome continues to alias PROS-001=WIN-004 and PROS-002=WIN-007 (tracked via restates_win in predictions.json).

progressive_disclosure.details: 333 <details> and 329 <summary> elements. 103 ps-tldr + 226 ks-tldr TLDRs; zero empty. All WINs have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict. 15 genuinely-prospective predictions; 14 have tldr field. One (MT-003 Schumann suppression G3 storm test) is missing tldr — moderate.

hardcoded_theme_colors.details: 2 hardcoded light colors in sections.json inline styles: part1.html uses background:#f5f5f5 in a monospace code block; part6.html uses background:#fff in an image-link container (patent US787412A). Both visibly break dark theme.

hits.context: font-family: monospace; background: #f5f5f5; padding: 1rem; margin: 0.5rem 0; border-left: 3px solid #666

discoverability.details: llms.txt (3245 bytes) contains review URL and dome model name; count '69 dome claims' matches base WIN count. robots.txt has Allow: / and Sitemap: directive. sitemap.xml is valid XML with urlset root and two <loc> entries (main page + llms.txt). Meta tags present in head: description, og:title, og:description, and <script type='application/ld+json'> with ClaimReview. uncounted-failures.json: 8 entries, all required fields present (id, dome_ref, dome_label, what_actually_happened), no duplicate IDs, all in FAIL-NNN format.

expansion_tracker.details: 166 items; next_id=168, max_id=167 (invariant OK). One ID gap: EXP-52 is missing (has been missing across prior reports — consistent, not a new collision). No orphaned output files. No phantom tracker entries. No stale pending (>48h). Section-new / category-proposal-writeup priority-queue coupling: 0 items need coupling — all coupled-category items are already integrated.

workspace_sync_skips.details: No direction-violation skips in last 24h. 14 mtime-guard skips (informational only — git version newer, normal operation). Top: category-proposals/CAT-005.json 3x, daily-report-2026-04-14T23-13.json 3x, review-state.json 3x.

digest_coverage.details: 281 review files, 196 unique base IDs (latest cycle), 282 processed, 1 unprocessed: WIN-015.c3.json (generated 2026-04-17T07:14Z, same run). Within normal pipeline lag — decider will pick it up next run. No prefix-filter regression.

broken_review_files.details: Digest regenerated via `node build-scripts/digest-reviews.js --workspace .` (exit 0). Zero parse errors. Under-covered processed reviews count is 188 — a known pipeline observation, outside integrity scope.

workspace_only_files.details: 1 curmudgeon review file exists on workspace but not in git: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-015.c3.json. Age ~1h45m (written 2026-04-17T07:14Z, this run 09:00Z). This is normal pipeline lag for change-detected reviews awaiting the next workspace-sync/decider cycle; flagged as informational rather than major because it matches the single unprocessed digest entry and is the newest file in the directory.

project_docs.details: All 33 file paths referenced in CLAUDE.md exist on disk. CLAUDE.md provides query commands for live counts (no hardcoded tallies — only the dome's own claimed count of 69 is mentioned, which is correct). Agent schedule table present. FUSE-staleness and ownership-table documentation up to date.

build_reproducibility.details: Cloned fresh into /tmp/dome-integrity-clone, ran `node build.js html`. Diff vs workspace docs/index.html: 0 lines. Published HTML exactly matches a fresh build from the committed data/sections sources. Clone removed after check.

issues_found.description: Hardcoded light-theme background color #f5f5f5 in a monospace code block inside sections.json part1.html. Renders as a white panel against the dark theme.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace background:#f5f5f5 with background:var(--card-bg) (or a dedicated code-block variable) so it inherits from the dark theme.

issues_found.description: Hardcoded background:#fff on image-link wrapper for the US787412A patent image in part6.html. Produces a bright white tile in the dark theme.
issues_found.suggested_fix: The #fff was likely chosen so the transparent patent PNG doesn't blend into the page. Options: (a) use background:var(--card-bg) and accept PNG transparency, (b) pre-flatten the image onto the card color, or (c) introduce a dedicated .patent-frame utility class and define its background in the stylesheet so all similar embeds update together.

issues_found.description: Prediction MT-003 ('Schumann suppression first test during G3+ storm (March 22, 2026)') is is_genuinely_prospective=true but has no tldr field. Every other genuinely-prospective prediction has one.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add a 2-sentence tldr. Suggested: 'Opportunistic G3 storm test registered 2026-03-23 to check whether Schumann power drops during major geomagnetic activity. Outcome disputed; author_status=unresolved.'

issues_found.description: detail_group 'WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063' does not mention sub-ID 057b, which is also a member of this group. Label is descriptive shorthand; sub-ID 057b is our tracking entry for a dome numbering collision.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Cosmetic. If the label is user-facing (rendered in the HTML), extend it to 'WIN-040-043, 057b, 059-061, 063'. Otherwise leave as-is.

informational_notes: WIN-015.c3.json: curmudgeon wrote a change-driven re-review ~1h45m before this integrity run. It is workspace-only until the next workspace-sync cycle commits it. If it is still missing from git in tomorrow's run, escalate to major.
Workspace-sync skip log shows 14 mtime-guard skips in 24h across 8 distinct paths — all normal (git is authoritative for those files).
EXP-52 remains a permanent tracker gap (already documented in prior reports). next_id=168 > max_id=167 invariant holds.

The digest tool reports 188 'under-covered processed' reviews with 1004 missing issues. This is a separate pipeline observation the decider/tinker track; it is outside integrity scope unless it grows sharply.
`node build.js html` in a fresh clone produces byte-identical docs/index.html — the published site is reproducible from committed sources alone.
summary: Overall PASS. Test suite green (3359/3359). Build reproducible: fresh clone produces byte-identical docs/index.html. Tab structure, nav chain, internal anchors, data-prose counts, WIN detail rendering, TLDR coverage, discoverability files (llms.txt/sitemap.xml/robots.txt/meta/ClaimReview), expansion tracker invariants, digest pipeline, and workspace-sync direction guard all clean. WIN number alignment with dome site confirmed (69 confirmed; PROS alias tracked). Moderate issues: two hardcoded light-theme colors in sections.json (part1.html #f5f5f5 code-block background, part6.html #fff patent-image frame); one prediction (MT-003) missing tldr. Informational: curmudgeon review WIN-015.c3.json written this morning is not yet committed to git but is within normal pipeline lag and is the single unprocessed entry in the digest.
